publications.title: Correlation between Optical and Electrical Properties of the Pulsed-Laser Deposited Amorphous Films of Germanium
publications.summary: Amorphous films of germanium (thicknesses 25 - 450 nm) were obtained on glass substrate at room temperature by the vacuum (residual gas pressure 3´10-6 mm Hg) pulsed-laser (Q-switched glass: Nd3+ laser (1.064 µm wavelength, 30 ns pulse duration, laser energy - 3 J per pulse, laser intensity on Ge target ~109 W/cm2)) deposition method. The comprehensive study of the optical and electrical properties of amorphous films of germanium showed the presence of two types (A and B) of films with different characteristics. With increasing of film thickness from 100 nm to 450 nm we observe the transition from the prevailing topological disorder (A- type) to the quantify disorder (B- type) in the region of 300-350 nm. Thicker films are characterized by a more homogeneous “network” of bonds, which minimizes the number of defects and localized states in the “forbidden” gap and, consequently, increases the resistivity and optical gap. An increase of the resistivity and optical gap is observed with decreasing film thickness (< 100 nm), which is explained by an increasing influence of natural oxidation of thin films. The results of temperature measurements for A- type films shows that the temperature dependence of resistivity is characterized by Mott's law (lnρ ~ T-1/4) in the temperature range of 100-240 K, while for B- type it indicates an activation character (lnR ~ ΔEa/kT) in the temperature range of 150-350 K.

publications.title: OPTICAL PROPERTIES OF THE PULSED-LASER DEPOSITED AMORPHOUS FILMS OF GERMANIUM
publications.source: International Journal of Modern Physics: Conference Series (IJMPCS)
publications.summary: Amorphous germanium (a-Ge) films (thickness 25-450 nm) were obtained on the glass substrates at room temperature by the vacuum (3·10^-6 mm Hg) pulsed laser (Q-switched glass: Nd3+ laser: wavelength — 1.06 μm, pulse duration — 30 ns, intensity in the Ge target irradiation zone — ~10^9 W/cm2) deposition method in non-equilibrium conditions of growth. The optical properties of fabricated films were studied in the range of 400-1000 nm using a spectrometer Filmetrics F20. It was shown that the optical properties of a-Ge films are explained by the Tauc model for amorphous semiconductors. It was also established that the optical gap of films depends on their thickness.

publications.title: Vis-NIR characteristics of amorphous germanium films and electrical properties of a-Ge/p-Si heterostructures obtained by pulsed-laser deposition method
publications.source: JOURNAL OF CONTEMPORARY PHYSICS (ARMENIAN ACADEMY OF SCIENCES)
publications.summary: Optical properties of a-Ge films (glass substrate) and electrical properties of a-Ge/p-Si heterostructures obtained by the pulsed-laser deposition method have been studied. It is shown that optical properties of a-Ge films can be well explained by the Tauc model for amorphous semiconductors. The dependence of optical gap on the film thickness is obtained for a-Ge films. Forward-bias current-voltage characteristics of the a-Ge/p-Si heterostructures are satisfactorily approximated by the relation for current density J = CV^m , where m varies from 1.45 to 1.95 depending on the applied forward bias and a-Ge film thickness. Also, for the mentioned heterostructure (a-Ge film thickness is 400 nm) nearly quadratic dependence of the current density is observed, which indicates the predominance of the space-charge-limited current.

recommendationsReceived.name: Arthur Evseev
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Software Engineer
recommendationsReceived.context: Arthur worked with Arsén Y. on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description:
I worked with Arsen at Microsoft in the group focused on building conversational engine for multiple Microsoft initiatives.
It was certainly a pleasure to work with him: Arsen has impressive technical background and exceptional communication capabilities, he was never satisfied with status quo and capable to drive complex and challenging projects.

I would be very happy to work with Arsen again!

recommendationsReceived.name: Chen Dai
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Software Engineer at Amazon Web Services (AWS)
recommendationsReceived.context: Chen worked with Arsén Y. on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: I worked with Arsen at AWS Elasticsearch for half a year. In this short time, Arsen made a good impression with his work in our SQL project. He took ownership of his project and had insisted high standard all the time. Particularly, he paid attention to every detail in code and shared his knowledge in Java and OO design. I learned from him in many code reviews we had together. And he is a good teammate. I enjoyed working with him in the same team.

recommendationsReceived.name: Steve Blum
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Engineering Manager | Customer Obsessed | Product Driven | Data Geek | Big Family Dad | On a Mission
recommendationsReceived.description:
Arsen worked on my team at Amazon for a year and a half.  From my experience over that time, he is very good at taking complex problems and breaking them down into understandable chunks that can be worked on.  

Arsen takes ownership of the scope of work that he is given and makes sure that all aspects are accounted for, and all interested parties are involved and have a say.  He cares about the work and the products that are produced, and sets high standards for himself and his teammates.

recommendationsReceived.name: Jyh-Liang Yeh
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: If you have a T-Mobile web account, you are using my team's microservices.
recommendationsReceived.description: I worked with Arsén at Amazon during his tenure at PeopleTech.  He is a methodical and diligent engineer who takes high ownership of his projects and raises gaps in a respectful manner.  He is also a team player, has a unique flair for office humor, and is an awesome lunch-buddy ensuring his teammates are well nourished.

recommendationsReceived.name: Arghya ‎
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: SWE @ Meta Ads
recommendationsReceived.context: Arghya worked with Arsén Y. on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: Arsen has a very strong knowledge-base in computer science fundamentals. He is a solid engineer with strong technical acumen. It was great to have him as part of the Arcadia Team and then Developer Platform team in Microsoft. He always offered different approaches and perspectives to many difficult design problems we have faced and solved together.

recommendationsReceived.name: Shalini J.
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Engineering Manager @ Google | Cloud Storage & Computing | Columbia University
recommendationsReceived.context: Shalini worked with Arsén Y. on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: I worked with Arsen on my time on the Windows Bridge for Android project- We worked together on the Azure cloud service for one of the components; In my time working with him I noticed Arsen to be a fast learner, curious with lots of valid questions that would often result in me re-thinking the logic behind some of my own choices even if they were 'best practices' in- both in the design + coding aspects of development. Also open to constructive feedback and provided constructive feedback in return (design + code reviews); A fun teammate to work with and generally a nice person and of course goes without saying, a hardworker and will deliver on his projects.  Arsen will be an asset to any team he works on!

recommendationsReceived.name: Sapon Tanachaiwiwat
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Vice President of Engineering, Pricing and Data @ Vista | Data, AI/ML, Analytics Leadership, Solution Architecture
recommendationsReceived.description: Arsen has shown a passion to develop high quality software and provided significant contribution toward our current project especially in map-related components. His critical thinking skills also benefited our project greatly.  It has been a real pleasure working with him.

recommendationsReceived.name: Arsen Harutyunyan
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Software Engineer, MED-EL
recommendationsReceived.context: Arsen worked with Arsén Y. on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description:
It's a great pleasure to me to recommend Arsen. He is a highly talented and motivated software engineer. Arsen has already much experience, deep knowledge of the latest software development technologies. He is always open to new challenges. 
We worked together on many projects, he played key role in design and development of the UI layers. He is responsible, self-learning, performing his work with high quality and within timelines.
He has excellent communication and teamwork skills.

recommendationsReceived.name: Artak Begnazaryan
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Software Engineer at BMW Car IT GmbH
recommendationsReceived.context: Artak worked with Arsén Y. but on different teams
recommendationsReceived.description: Arsen is a smart person full of enthusiasm and motivation. His strong programming skills as well as advance mathematical and logical thinking help him to solve really hard tasks in an interesting and perfect way. I was really enjoying to ask him challenging problems and follow how he was applying different techniques and algorithms to solve it. He will not give up until he has the best solution for the problem. He is not satisfied from the huge amount of stuff he already knows and learns something new every day. He is a very nice person and a good team player and it's worth it to work with him in the same team.

recommendationsReceived.name: Artak Apinyan
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: LMTS Software Engineer, Salesforce (Tableau) | ex-Microsoft | ex-Bloomberg
recommendationsReceived.context: Artak was senior to Arsén Y. but didn't manage Arsén Y. directly
recommendationsReceived.description:
I have worked with Arsen for more than 2 years. Arsen is sharp thinking person with good professional background in software development area. Even at the beginning of his career he strove for a clean code and good design, which made him a professional very quickly. He is also good team player because of his exceptional communication skills and sense of humor.
It was really a pleasure to work with him. And now I am glad that we work together again.

recommendationsReceived.name: Diego Martirosyan
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Staff Software Engineer at Xilinx
recommendationsReceived.context: Diego worked with Arsén Y. on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description:
I am working with Arsen more than 3 years. He demonstrated profound knowledge in computer science. He always tries to understand the problem and suggest an optimal solution. Arsen has excellent communication skills and always opens for discussing any issue.
Also he is a good person and friend! I enjoyed working with him.

recommendationsReceived.name: Vasiliy Sitnikov
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Head of AI Platform Products
recommendationsReceived.context: Vasiliy was senior to Arsén Y. but didn't manage Arsén Y. directly
recommendationsReceived.description: Arsen is smart and friendly guy. He shows a result oriented approach in his work as member of AYYO test team.

recommendationsReceived.name: Arman Sahakyan
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Sr. Software Developer at Molsoft LLC
recommendationsReceived.context: Arman worked with Arsén Y. on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: It was my pleasure to work with Arsen. We were working in the same group in Synopsys Armenia. I can say that Arsen is very fast-oriented and always could find optimal solutions for the tasks he had been assigned to. He has excellent communication skills which is very important in the workplace. I enjoyed working with Arsen!

recommendations.name: Arthur Evseev
recommendations.subtitle: Software Engineer
recommendations.context: Arsén Y. worked with Arthur on the same team
recommendations.description: During the 2 years of collaboration with Arthur of which 1.5 was remote because of the pandemic, I learned that I could rely on his knowledge and experience with Cortana services whenever I needed help. He helped me onboard to the team when I joined, and from that moment on I constantly observed his readiness to guide and mentor others. Although we didn't work much on the same projects, we were still responsible for the health of all our services together, and Arthur effectively lead the efforts (cross-team) for making operations and on-call management much more streamlined and easier for all engineers. I think he always delivered more than what he planned for, and he strived for even more. It always brings peace of mind knowing that people like Arthur are on your team, because that's how teams teams deliver good products as well as better engineers.

recommendations.name: Arsen Harutyunyan
recommendations.subtitle: Software Engineer, MED-EL
recommendations.context: Arsén Y. worked with Arsen on the same team
recommendations.description:
I worked with Arsen on a couple of multi-tier web applications. He owned the database, and the service layer to interact with the database from the web app. Nevertheless, he never felt uncomfortable to go and check or fix something in other layers. He designed the service layer using best practices of the SoA, the API he provided had never depended on the UX specifics. It was specific to the business logic, but not to the presentation layer, and it was generic enough not to be modified any time there was a new requirement for the UI. I hardly remember a couple of cases where there was an issue in his code, but then again, he identified the root cause quickly and the fixes took him no more than an hour. He was an expert in Microsoft technologies, including C#, .NET, Silverlight, WCF, WPF, etc. There was not a single moment to make me doubt about Arsen's professional or technical competence.

Arsen was (and I am sure he continues to be) a perfect member of our team. Though he had much more experience in software development he never showed any disrespect to his less experienced colleagues and always tried to help anyone who was getting late from plans. Just before I left the company Arsen became the lead of our team, but his attitude to the teammates didn't change, he continued to work with even more vigor.

The company is judged by the people who build it and who work there. Arsen is one of those few people who made my experience with Ardinnotekh awesome.

recommendations.name: Artak Apinyan
recommendations.subtitle: LMTS Software Engineer, Salesforce (Tableau) | ex-Microsoft | ex-Bloomberg
recommendations.context: Arsén Y. worked with Artak but on different teams
recommendations.description:
I have worked with Artak at two different companies and I always observed the demonstration of his leadership skills: besides being a really good software developer, he also strove to create some process to follow, define milestones and timelines, prioritize the tasks as appropriate, so that he always could keep track of already done, being done, and to be done tasks. He did this not only for himself but always tried to spread the best practices of his experience across the whole team.
One more not less important thing I noticed was his sense of responsibility, he really cared about what he did and how he did, and he cared about what his team did and how, I remember him spending hours on more junior engineers' code just trying to make it correct or better. I am sure Artak kept on improving after I left the company, so now he is a better software engineer and a better leader.

recommendations.name: Artak Begnazaryan
recommendations.subtitle: Senior Software Engineer at BMW Car IT GmbH
recommendations.context: Arsén Y. worked with Artak but on different teams
recommendations.description:
During one-year period of work with Artak I learnt many surprisingly good things about him. He not only managed to complete all his tasks in time and in a good quality, but also kept up with the most contemporary technologies, at least he had to know about their existence. He enjoyed working on projects which made him learn new stuff. Though we did not work on the same part of the project, but we had some discussions on different issues (mainly concerning C++, design and algorithms), and both his knowledge and the way of thinking were impressive.

What I like very much in people is being happy to come to work, feeling responsible for completing their job in time and well, and being happy to go home after work. So this was what Artak actually did. Those were really good times, and I will always be glad to work with such people in the same team.

recommendations.name: Diego Martirosyan
recommendations.subtitle: Senior Staff Software Engineer at Xilinx
recommendations.context: Arsén Y. worked with Diego on the same team
recommendations.description: Diego is always one step ahead of those responsibilities and duties his position implies. I admired his proficiency in C++, object-oriented design, and his great responsibility for everything he did. One more remarkable quality is planning/time management which he also excelled at. I won't hesitate to recommend Diego as an experienced and proficient C++ software developer/architect for any appropriate position.

recommendations.name: Arman Sahakyan
recommendations.subtitle: Sr. Software Developer at Molsoft LLC
recommendations.context: Arsén Y. worked with Arman on the same team
recommendations.description: Besides the qualities of a professional developer Arman also owns the irreplaceable ones to precisely understand and interpret product requirements, and also the responsibility and punctuality in meeting the strongest deadlines. It is my pleasure to recommend Arman to having business with.

summary: Arsén Y. M. is currently a senior/principal software engineer at Microsoft (a large public enterprise) working on cloud and DevOps security (Defender for DevOps / Defender for Cloud). His LinkedIn activity mixes hands‑on technical content (tutorial video series #programmingWithArsen, debugging tips, sample code and Visual Studio guidance) with community building—posting job and internship opportunities, promoting Armenian tech education, and helping peers find roles. He writes in both Armenian and English, alternating practical how‑tos (binary numbers, functions, control flow, scripting for diagnostics) with reflective leadership and career commentary—calling out social media myths about success and offering pragmatic productivity advice. Arsén demonstrates technical depth while staying approachable: many posts are step‑by‑step educational pieces, short experiments or scripts, and recruiter‑style job announcements. Recurring themes include DevOps/security, programming fundamentals pedagogy, community support for Armenian initiatives (ACA, COAF, SASTIC), and pragmatic use of AI assistants (ChatGPT/Copilot) to speed tasks. Overall his voice is a blend of educator, problem solver, and empathetic industry insider who leverages his enterprise experience to uplift learners and colleagues.
highlights: DevOps & Cloud Security (Defender for DevOps / Defender for Cloud)
